Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2015 Africa Cup of Nations squads
← 2013
2017 →
International football competition

The 2015 Africa Cup of Nations was an international football tournament hosted by Equatorial Guinea from 17 January to 8 February 2015.

Group A

[edit]

Equatorial Guinea

[edit]

Coach: Esteban Becker

The final squad was announced on 8 January 2015.[1]

  9. ^ "Kalaba to lead Zambia at Nations Cup". bbc.com. 7 January 2015. Retrieved 7 January 2015.
  10. ^ "CAN 2015 : La liste des 26 joueurs convoqués pour la préparation de la CAN" (in French). ftf.org.tn. Archived from the original on 27 December 2014. Retrieved 27 December 2014.
  11. ^ "Listes des 23 joueurs retenus pour la CAN" (in French). ftf.org.tn. 7 January 2015. Archived from the original on 8 January 2015. Retrieved 7 January 2015.
  12. ^ Fakhreddine Ben Youssef was originally named in the squad but sustained an injury before the tournament. He was replaced by Rjaïbi. "Convocation de Edam Rjaibi et Ahmed Akaichi pour la CAN" (in French). ftf.org.tn. Archived from the original on 19 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015.
  13. ^ Saber Khalifa was originally named in the squad but sustained an injury before the tournament. He was replaced by Akaïchi. "Convocation de Edam Rjaibi et Ahmed Akaichi pour la CAN" (in French). ftf.org.tn. Archived from the original on 19 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015.
  14. ^ "CONVOCADOS CAN 2015" (in Portuguese). Cape Verdean Football Federation. Archived from the original on 8 April 2015. Retrieved 26 December 2014.

  16. ^ "Grant names 31-man provisional squad for Afcon". ghanafa.org. 24 December 2014. Archived from the original on 24 December 2014. Retrieved 26 December 2014.
  17. ^ "Ghana names final squad for AFCON 2015". ghanafa.org. 7 January 2015. Archived from the original on 8 January 2015. Retrieved 8 January 2015.
  18. ^ "CAN 2015 : La liste des 23 joueurs retenus" (in French). faf.dz. 16 December 2014. Archived from the original on 16 December 2014. Retrieved 16 December 2014.
  19. ^ Essaïd Belkalem was originally named in the squad but sustained an injury before the tournament. He was replaced by Cadamuro. "Algérie : Belkalem et Abeïd forfaits pour la CAN" (in French). cafonline.com. 4 January 2015. Retrieved 4 January 2015.
  20. ^ Mohamed Zemmamouche was originally named in the squad but sustained an injury before the tournament. He was replaced by Si Mohamed. "CAN 2015 : Si Mohammed remplace Zemmamouche, blessé" (in French). faf.dz. 14 January 2015. Archived from the original on 18 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015.
  21. ^ Mehdi Abeid was originally named in the squad but sustained an injury before the tournament. He was replaced by Kashi. "Algérie : Belkalem et Abeïd forfaits pour la CAN" (in French). cafonline.com. 4 January 2015. Retrieved 4 January 2015.
  22. ^ "Mashaba announces provisional squad for AFCON 2015". safa.net. 18 December 2014. Archived from the original on 19 December 2014. Retrieved 18 December 2014.
  23. ^ "South Africa name squad for Africa Cup of Nations". cafonline.com. 31 December 2014. Retrieved 2 January 2015.
  24. ^ Patrick Phungwayo was originally named in the squad but sustained an injury before the tournament. He was replaced by Gcaba. "Gcaba called up to replace Phungwayo". safa.net. Archived from the original on 19 December 2014. Retrieved 12 January 2015.
  25. ^ "CAN 2015: Sénégal, Giresse publie une liste élargie de 28 joueurs sans Demba Ba" (in French). africatopsports.com. 26 December 2014. Retrieved 26 December 2014.
  26. ^ "Mané est bien dans la liste" (in French). francefootball.fr. 8 January 2015. Retrieved 8 January 2015.
  27. ^ "Les 23 d'Hervé Renard" (in French). fedivoir.com. 29 December 2014. Archived from the original on 29 December 2014. Retrieved 29 December 2014.
  28. ^ "Quatre locaux dans la pré-liste de Kasperczak" (in French). cafonline.com. 27 December 2014. Retrieved 27 December 2014.
  29. ^ "Maïga et Abdou Traoré de retour". L'Équipe (in French). 28 December 2014. Retrieved 28 December 2014.
  30. ^ Cheick Diabaté was originally named in the squad but sustained an injury before the tournament. He was replaced by Traoré. "M. Traoré remplace Diabaté" (in French). francefootball.fr. 2 January 2015. Retrieved 2 January 2015.
  31. ^ "LISTE DES JOUEURS CONVOQUES POUR LE STAGE DE PREPARATION DE LA CAN 2015" (in French). Cameroonian Football Federation. 24 December 2014. Archived from the original on 27 December 2014. Retrieved 26 December 2014.

  33. ^ "CAN 2015: Les 23 Guinéens pour la Guinée équatoriale" (in French). africatopsports.com. 30 December 2014. Retrieved 30 December 2014.
  34. ^ Bouna Sarr was originally named in the squad but refused his call-up. He was replaced by Landel. "Nations Cup 2015: Bouna Sarr turns down Guinea". bbc.com. 4 January 2015. Retrieved 4 January 2015.

Notes
The 2021 and 2023 tournaments were actually held in 2022 and 2024 respectively.
There were no 1957 and 1959 qualifications as places were given by invitation only.
The "finals" articles for 1959 and 1976 are about the decisive matches of final group stages.
